name: Camp Hero Facility
description: Camp Hero, also known as the Montauk Air Force Station, is a former United States Air Force base located in Montauk, New York. While officially decommissioned, it is a prominent subject of numerous conspiracy theories, primarily revolving around purported clandestine experiments conducted within its underground facilities. These theories, popularized by figures like Preston Nichols and the Montauk Project series of books, claim that Camp Hero was the site of experiments involving mind control, time travel, psychic abilities, and contact with extraterrestrial beings. Proponents often cite alleged recovered memories, anecdotal accounts from supposed former employees, and unusual electromagnetic anomalies detected in the area as evidence. Some theories also suggest a connection to the Philadelphia Experiment. The mainstream perspective views these claims as unsubstantiated and lacking credible evidence. Skeptics attribute the theories to misinterpretations of historical events, psychological phenomena such as false memories, and the creative imagination of individuals involved. The physical infrastructure of the base, including its radar tower and underground tunnels, has fueled speculation, but official explanations attribute these structures to military purposes during the Cold War, particularly related to radar surveillance and defense. The site is now a state park, open to the public, further diminishing the plausibility of ongoing covert activities. While the history of Camp Hero as a military installation is well-documented, the conspiracy theories surrounding it remain a significant part of American folklore and alternative history.
subtitle: Site of alleged mind control experiments.

claims.claim: Camp Hero was used for mind control experiments.
claims.evidence: Alleged recovered memories of individuals claiming to have been subjects; anecdotal accounts.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable evidence; the fallibility of memory; potential for suggestion and confabulation.

claims.claim: Camp Hero was involved in time travel research.
claims.evidence: Testimonies of individuals claiming to have participated in time travel projects; alleged temporal anomalies.
claims.criticism: Absence of scientific basis for time travel claims; inconsistencies in accounts; lack of physical proof.

claims.claim: Camp Hero housed a portal to other dimensions or extraterrestrial beings.
claims.evidence: Unexplained electromagnetic anomalies; alleged sightings of unusual phenomena; accounts of contact with non-human entities.
claims.criticism: Misinterpretation of natural phenomena; lack of credible scientific evidence; potential for hoaxes and misidentification.

keyFigures.name: Preston Nichols
keyFigures.role: Author and proponent of the Montauk Project theories.

keyFigures.name: Peter Moon
keyFigures.role: Author who collaborated with Preston Nichols on the Montauk Project series.

keyFigures.name: Jacques Vallée
keyFigures.role: Scientist and ufologist who has critically examined the Montauk Project claims.

mythologyConnections: Connections to folklore about secret government projects
References to alchemic pursuits of immortality and power

timeline.event: Camp Hero established as a military base during World War II.
Camp Hero served as an Air Force Station during the Cold War.
Official decommissioning of Camp Hero as an active military base.
Emergence and popularization of the Montauk Project conspiracy theories.
Camp Hero State Park opens to the public.
